finding_results.notes: Three abstract, essay-register phrases replaced with concrete, pressured, character-true speech. (1) 'Commentary on the medium' replaced with 'Like, your mom leaves voicemails. Bill collectors. That's it.' — concrete examples in Nate's list-building mode, followed by 'that's the joke, that's the whole —' with an unfinished restart. (2) 'the language — ... the language we use' replaced with 'the things we say — ... the shit we say to each other' — colloquial, profanity-true for Nate's moderate-high profile under pressure, removes the abstractly analytical framing. (3) 'That's the dialect' replaced with 'Every guy in every league says that stuff' — appeals to concrete shared behavior instead of labeling it with a sociolinguistic term. All three fixes preserve the surrounding pressure cadence, the accidental first-person slip at 'when I said,' and the full confession arc through the deletion admission.

summary: Single finding addressed across three surgical edits targeting Nate's confession lead-in (lines 135, 139, 153). Removed two essay-register phrases ('Commentary on the medium,' 'That's the dialect') and one abstractly analytical framing ('the language we use') that momentarily made Nate sound like a media critic diagnosing his own speech. Replacements use concrete examples, list-building, mild profanity, and unfinished restarts consistent with Nate's style-bible voice under interrogation pressure. The confession arc, accidental self-incrimination, and all chapter hooks are preserved intact.
